## 0.1.0 (2026-05-29)

## Release Notes

Release auto-merge now merges approved, green release PRs automatically with fine-grained opt-in control.

## ✨ What's New

### Automatic release PR merging
Release PRs no longer require manual merging once they're approved and all checks pass. The system monitors PR events and automatically merges when conditions are met, eliminating the wait between approval and publish. This is especially valuable for teams managing multiple packages in a monorepo, where each package gets its own release PR.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#107: Add release-automerge composite action with downstream sync workflow](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/107)
</details>

### Granular opt-in controls
Teams can now enable auto-merge at the repository level or per individual package. Set `release.automerge: true` in your root `package.json` to enable for all packages, or configure it per workspace member to opt specific packages in or out. This flexibility lets teams gradually adopt automation or exclude packages that need manual oversight.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#112: Gate release auto-merge behind a release.automerge flag in package.json](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/112)
</details>

### Unified release pipeline sync
The complete release pipeline (create, auto-merge, and publish workflows) now syncs to downstream repositories as a unit, ensuring consistent behavior across your organization's repos.

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Check status aggregation improvements
The system now correctly handles GitHub Actions that were cancelled without running, preventing false negatives when evaluating whether all checks have passed. This ensures release PRs merge reliably even when non-critical workflows are cancelled.

## ⚙️ Configuration Required

### Enable auto-merge flag
Auto-merge is disabled by default. To enable it, add the following to your `package.json`:

```json
{
  "release": {
    "automerge": true
  }
}
```

For monorepos, you can set this at the root level (applies to all packages) or configure per workspace member. Member-level settings override the root default.

## 📚 Documentation & Settings Updates

### Release configuration documentation
Added comprehensive documentation for the `release.automerge` flag, including examples of repository-wide and per-package configuration patterns.

### Auto-merge flow documentation
New documentation explains the complete auto-merge flow, including trigger events, safety checks, and integration with the broader release pipeline.
